message:
feat(termwiz): add RenderUploadSnapshot with global tracking for render diagnostics

Introduce a global render upload snapshot mechanism that captures the
dirty-region mode, region counts, and cell counts from each render pass,
making render performance observable without per-frame logging.

New types and API:
- RenderUploadMode enum (Rects vs Tiles{width,height}) — public mirror
  of the internal DirtyRegionMode for external consumption
- RenderUploadSnapshot struct — captures mode, widget_regions,
  widget_cells, frame_regions, frame_cells from a single render pass
- From<DirtyRegionMode> impl for RenderUploadMode conversion
- global_render_upload_snapshot() — reads the latest snapshot from a
  process-wide OnceLock<RwLock<Option<RenderUploadSnapshot>>>
- update_global_render_upload_snapshot() — called internally after
  each render pass to publish the latest snapshot
- Ui::last_render_upload_snapshot() — per-instance accessor

Additional changes:
- Replace deprecated SequenceNo::max_value() with SequenceNo::MAX
- Fix tile-mode second-render assertions: after the first render
  uploads all dirty tiles, subsequent renders still detect tile-level
  dirtiness (widget_dirty_tiles >= 1) even when no rect-level changes
  occurred, because the tile granularity captures sub-rect mutations
- New test: render_modes_preserve_frame_semantics_under_churn —
  verifies rect and tile render modes produce identical visible frames,
  cursor state, and update flags over 8 churn cycles
- New test: global_render_upload_snapshot_tracks_latest_render_pass —
  verifies the global snapshot is populated after tile-mode rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
